onboarding.welcomeSubtitle: Your personal guide to household finances
onboarding.philosophy: Sthedisia helps you give every euro a purpose. Instead of tracking where money went, we plan ahead where it will go. Simple, no complicated jargon.
onboarding.nameUsage: We'll use this name in the app
onboarding.step1Subtitle: Enter your total net monthly income (salary, freelance, etc.)
onboarding.step2Title: How many people in your household?
onboarding.step2Subtitle: This helps us tailor advice for your household
onboarding.step3Subtitle: Choose one or more goals you'd like to achieve
onboarding.step4Subtitle: Add the accounts you use (bank, cash, cards)
onboarding.step5Subtitle: Start tracking your finances and take control of your budget

auth.createPinHint: Choose a 4-digit PIN to protect your data

dashboard.title: Home
dashboard.howAmIDoing: How am I doing this month?
dashboard.statusWarning: Heads up — you're approaching limits in some categories.
dashboard.statusOver: You've exceeded your budget in some categories.
dashboard.noTransactionsHint: Add your first transaction by tapping the "Transactions" tab and start tracking your finances!
dashboard.limitsExplanation: The budget uses the 50/30/20 rule recommended by financial experts (Elizabeth Warren):
dashboard.limitsNeeds: 50% of income — needs (housing 27%, food 13%, transport 5%, utilities 3%, health 2%)
dashboard.limitsWants: 30% of income — wants (entertainment 10%, clothing 5%, personal 5%, education 5%, gifts 5%)
dashboard.limitsSavings: 20% of income — savings and debt repayment (10% each)
dashboard.limitsCustomize: You can customize these limits in the Budget tab → Envelope mode.

transactions.title: Transactions
transactions.noTransactionsHint: Add your first transaction by tapping the + button!
transactions.fillRequired: Fill in amount, category and account.

budget.title: Budget
budget.setupSubtitle: Give every euro a purpose! Choose a method that works for you.
budget.envelopeExplain: Imagine putting every euro in a separate envelope. Each envelope has its purpose — rent, food, fun... When the envelope is empty, that's it for the month!
budget.rule503020Explain: Simple rule: 50% for needs (rent, bills, food), 30% for wants (entertainment, clothing), 20% for savings and debt.
budget.regenerateConfirm: This will reset all categories to recommended percentages based on the 50/30/20 rule. Continue?
budget.unbudgetedHint: These categories have expenses but no budget set.
budget.noPreviousBudget: No budget from last month to copy.

goals.title: Goals
goals.noGoalsHint: Set your first goal and start saving for something special!
goals.monthlyNeeded: You'll need to save approximately {{amount}} per month for this goal.
goals.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the goal "{{name}}"?
goals.netWorthExplain: Net worth = total balance of all accounts minus all debts. The goal is for this number to grow month by month. It's the most important indicator of your financial health.
goals.emergencyFundExplain: It's recommended to have savings covering 3-6 months of your expenses. This is your financial cushion for unexpected situations like job loss, car breakdown, or medical costs.

accounts.title: Finances
accounts.deleteAccountConfirm: Are you sure? All transactions linked to this account will be deleted. This cannot be undone.
accounts.deleteDebtConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"?
accounts.noSubscriptions: No subscriptions detected. Add recurring transactions to see them here.
accounts.subscriptionHint: Automatically detected from your recurring payments.
accounts.snowballDesc: Smallest debt first — close debts faster, greater motivation
accounts.avalancheDesc: Highest interest first — mathematically optimal, less total interest

recurring.title: Recurring
recurring.noPaymentsHint: Add bills, subscriptions and regular payments to track them easily.
recurring.invalidInput: Enter a description and valid amount.

settings.title: Settings
settings.logoutConfirm: Are you sure you want to log out? Your data remains saved.
settings.footer:
Sthedisia v1.0.0
Your data is stored locally on your device.

reports.title: Reports
reports.forecastHint: Forecast is based on average income and expenses over the last 90 days. Actual results may vary.

advisor.title: Advisor
advisor.checkinDescription: Take 2 minutes to reflect on your finances this week
advisor.checkinHint: Weekly check-ins help you stay aware of your financial habits and make small adjustments before problems grow.
advisor.learnDescription: Short articles for better understanding of finances
advisor.termsDescription: Financial terms explained in simple language

household.title: Household
household.noHousehold: You're not part of a household
household.noHouseholdDesc: Create a new household or join an existing one using an invite code.
household.joinError: Invite code not found. Check the code and try again.
household.inviteCodeHint: Share this code with your partner so they can join your household.
household.leaveConfirmMessage: Your data stays, but you won't see other members' balances anymore.
household.syncDescription: Export your data as a JSON file and share it with your partner via Google Drive, AirDrop, or other methods.
household.importConfirmMessage: This will add your partner's data to your household. Your existing data won't be deleted.
household.importError: Invalid file format or import error.
household.syncHint: Tip: Create a shared folder on Google Drive and save exported files there. That way you both have access to the latest data.

list: Before buying something, ask yourself: 'Do I need it or want it?' This simple distinction can save hundreds of euros monthly.
Try the '24-hour rule' — when you see something expensive, wait a day. You'll often realize you don't actually need it.
Reviewing subscriptions monthly can reveal services you don't use. The average person spends €40-60 on forgotten subscriptions!
Even €50 per month in savings is €600 per year. Small steps create big results.
Cooking at home instead of ordering delivery can save €200-400 monthly for a two-person household.
Automated savings work wonders — set up automatic transfers to savings right after payday.
Compare prices at two or three stores before bigger purchases. Online comparison tools save both time and money.
An emergency fund covering 3 months of expenses is your financial cushion. Start small and build gradually.
Make a list before going to the store. Impulse purchases account for about 20% of total food spending.
Annual subscriptions are usually 20-40% cheaper than monthly ones. Check if you can switch some.
When you get a raise, 'pay yourself first' — put at least half the raise into savings before getting used to higher spending.
Tap water instead of bottled can save €200-500 per year. Plus, it's better for the environment!
Set goals you're excited about. Saving for a vacation is more motivating than 'saving just in case'.
Track expenses for at least 3 months to see real spending patterns. We're often surprised by which categories cost the most.
Share expenses with your partner transparently. A shared budget reduces stress and money conflicts.
LED bulbs use 75% less electricity. Replacing all bulbs can save €50-100 per year.
Plan meals on Sunday for the whole week. You'll waste less food and order less delivery.
Before a big purchase, ask: 'How many hours do I need to work to earn this?' Puts the price in perspective.
Cashback cards can return 1-3% of spending. On an annual basis, that could be €100-300.
Don't compare yourself to others on social media. Most people only show the nice side of finances.
A 50% discount on something you don't need isn't savings — it's an expense. Only buy what you'd buy at full price too.
Energy rating of appliances matters. An A+++ fridge uses half the electricity of an old model.
Once a month, review all automatic charges. You'd be surprised how many services you forget to cancel.
A shared budget doesn't mean equal amounts — it means proportional to income. It's fair when everyone contributes according to their means.
Investing in education (courses, books, conferences) has the highest return — in yourself.
